ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Close Of Estate Set For 2/4/20.
Off Calendar Sep-08-2020 Off Calendar

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Sep-08-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gilbert Jay appeared by telephone for Gordon Cheung and Herman Kwan (Co-Executors).

Mr. Jay stated that Herman Kwan is at his office. The Court stated that it received the written response to the order to show cause that was filed on August 27, 2020, noted that it suspended the Co-Executors and stated that the response did not request reinstatement. Mr. Jay sated that he is requesting reinstatement of the Co-Executors, and that his Associate hand delivered an order for reinstatement on Friday downstairs and one in the drop box in front of Dept. 204. Mr. Jay also stated that he filed for termination of the proceeding on August 27th. The Court reinstates the Co-Executors. Mr. Jay is to be open to receiving another request from the Court in the event the order can't be located. The order to appear and order to show cause is off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Close Of Estate Set For 2/4/20.
Off Calendar Aug-11-2020 Continued To Sep-08-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Gilbert Y. Jay appearing by CourtCall for Gordon Ho Cheung and Herman Kwan (Co-Executors).

The Court stated that before it today is status on close of the estate, that the matter was before this Court four or five weeks ago, and that Mr. Jay was to provide the Court additional information. Mr. Jay provided a brief explanation to the Court noting that the related case was denied, that jurisdiction to the grantor trust was denied, that there are two battling heirs, and that the will states that it is to devolve to trust for distribution. Mr. Jay also stated that there is no access to the personal property, that it has been determined by his client that there is no market value to the asset, that Mrs. Yee was 95 years old, that there may be sentimental value only to the two battling heirs, that he had spoken with the probate referee as to how to appraise the asset, and that the two battling heirs needed to sit down to discuss the asset. The Court noted that this was deja vu, that it wants a petition on report on status of administration on file, and that it is suspending the powers of the Co-Executors Gordon Ho Cheung and Herman Kwan. The status on close of the estate before the Court today is continued to September 8, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. An Order to Show Cause is to issue to Gordon Ho Cheung and Herman Kwan to appear on September 8, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they should not be removed and why the estate should not be terminated. A response to the Order to Show Cause is to be on file by September 1, 2020. The Court stated that it hopes that a petition will be on file by that date and that the Court expects an explanation as to why the estate has not closed. Gordon Ho Cheung and Herman Kwan may appear by CourtCall or by Zoom on September 8, 2020. Mr. Jay argued in support of the Court not suspending the Co-Executors noting that he personally had an unexpected death of a patriarch in the family and that he had hoped the Court would continue the matter instead of the Court proceeding with this rash act. The Court stated that it did not consider its order suspending the Co-Executors a rash act, that it was sorry for Mr. Jay's loss, that it needs to move this matter forward, that it has issued an Order to Show Cause, and that Mr. Jay knows what he needs to do.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Close Of Estate Set For 2/4/20.
Off Calendar Jul-07-2020 Continued To Aug-11-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gilbert Jay for Co-Executors.

The Court noted that the inventory and a petition have not been filed, and asks for status of the estate. Mr. Jay stated that this is a difficult case, that it may be a zero-asset estate and that the only asset is a personal asset for which the Beneficiaries have restricted access. Mr. Jay also spoke of the related trust (PTR-19-303092) Petition for Court Recognition of Existence of Grantor Trust and Judicial Oversight of the Administration that was denied by Judge Busch and the Court refused to accept jurisdiction of, and stated that two primary Beneficiaries are battling between this Court and Alameda. Status on close of estate is continued to August 11, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Mr. Jay that it does not anticipate any further dates on this.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Close Of Estate Set For 2/4/20.
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Jul-07-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Close Of Estate Set For 2/4/20.
Off Calendar Mar-10-2020 Continued To Apr-14-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gilbert Jay for Gordon Cheung and Herman and Herman Kwan (Co-Executors).

Mr. Jay stated that a Petition for Court Recognition of Existence of Grantor Trust and Judicial Oversight of the Administration was filed and heard, and that the Court refused to accept jurisdiction, that two primary Beneficiaries are battling between this Court and Alameda, that the only asset is personal property and that it is impossible to file an inventory and appraisal at this time. The Court informed Mr. Jay that he should have filed a petition for approval of report on status of administration. Status on close of estate is continued to April 14, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Jay is to file his petition by April 7, 2020. The order to appear is off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-10-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Close Of Estate Set For 2/4/20.
Off Calendar Feb-04-2020 Continued To Mar-10-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Executors and the Attorney to appear on March 10, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why the inventory and appraisal, and a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration have not been filed. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
